data.title: Titan Machinery adjusts international revenue outlook while targeting $100M inventory reduction in fiscal 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Titan Machinery (TITN) Q1 2026

Management View

  • President and CEO Bryan Knutson emphasized disciplined execution in inventory reduction and customer care strategy, stating, "Our first quarter results demonstrated our ability to advance our short term goals in a challenging market environment. And while headwinds persist across the agricultural sector, our team remains focused on continuing to execute upon our initiative to optimize inventory and navigate through the trough of the cycle."
  • Knutson noted progress in inventory management, reporting, "total inventories were $1.1 billion as of April 30, 2025, essentially flat compared to fiscal 2025 year end. This is very much in line with our previously communicated expectations as we've been receiving pre-sold units from the factory for delivery to customers in the first half of the year while simultaneously taking in trades as we deliver those new units to customers."
  • The company announced the acquisition of Farmers Implement & Irrigation, expanding its New Holland presence in Eastern South Dakota.
  • CFO Bo Larsen reported, "Total revenue was $594.3 million compared to $628.7 million in the prior year period, reflecting a 5.5% decrease in same store sales driven by the factors that Bryan discussed earlier. Gross profit for the first quarter was $90.9 million compared to $121.8 million in the prior year period and gross profit margin was 15.3%."

Outlook

  • Knutson reiterated a cautious outlook for the back half of the year in domestic agriculture, with "lower visibility and currently sluggish order activity." He added, "Farmers remain in a wait and see mode with near term sentiment hinging on commodity prices, moisture levels and the potential of government farm aid."
  • CFO Larsen stated, "we are reiterating from an adjusted loss per diluted share perspective but modifying in terms of revenue modeling assumptions for our international segments."
  • For fiscal 2026, revenue in the domestic agriculture segment is expected to be down 20% to 25%, construction segment down 5% to 10%, European segment up 23% to 28%, and Australia segment down 20% to 25%. Consolidated full year equipment margin is projected to be approximately 8%.
  • Larsen confirmed, "the $100 million of additional equipment inventory reductions we discussed last quarter remains our target with most of that reduction expected to come in the second half of this fiscal year."

Financial Results

  • The company reported a net loss for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 of $13.2 million or ($0.58) per diluted share, compared to last year's first quarter net income of $9.4 million or $0.41 per diluted share.
  • Operating expenses were $96.4 million, a 2.8% decrease year-over-year, with floorplan and other interest expense at $11.1 million, down 15.3% sequentially.
  • Agriculture segment realized a same store sales decrease of 14.1% to $384.4 million and pretax loss of $12.8 million. Construction segment same store sales increased 0.9% to $72.1 million with a pretax loss of $4.2 million. European segment sales rose 44.2% to $93.9 million with pretax income of $4.7 million. Australia segment same store sales decreased 1% to $44 million with pretax loss of $0.6 million.
  • Cash was $22 million and adjusted debt to tangible net worth ratio stood at 1.8 as of April 30, 2025.

Q&A

  • Liam Burke, B. Riley Securities: Asked about agricultural sector outlook and construction sector sentiment. Knutson responded that government payments are providing some stability, but "the potential of government farm aid... is still very much an unknown." For construction, he added, "Their attitudes are becoming more positive... It's certainly not what it was the past two years that's for sure."
  • Ted Jackson, Northland Securities: Inquired about USDA support and ag market trough. Larsen explained that most payments are for natural disasters and specifics remain to be seen. Knutson clarified the use of "trough" aligns with historically low industry volumes, "it certainly aligns with it is at or slightly below the trough of the last couple of decades."
  • Mig Dobre, Baird: Sought clarity on inventory targets by region, European revenue guidance, and domestic ag margin improvement. Knutson detailed that $100 million inventory reduction is minimum, mostly focused on ag and Europe. For Romania, EU funds are driving the increased European segment guidance. On margin improvement, Knutson cited ongoing inventory optimization.
  • Ben Klieve, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about OEM initiatives and their impact. Knutson said OEMs may use financing and incentives to support demand; Larsen noted any further support could help with margin and inventory reduction. Klieve also asked about parts and service margins; Larsen expects "similar levels, slightly positive levels that still remains the expectation."
  • Matthew Raab, Craig Hallum Capital Group: Asked about service gross margin trends and traffic. Larsen indicated expectations for flattish parts and service revenue and slightly positive margin trends.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious and probing sentiment, repeatedly questioning the stability of government support, ag sector trough, and sustainability of inventory reductions. Tone was slightly negative, especially regarding agricultural demand visibility and profitability.
  • Management maintained a neutral to slightly defensive tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing disciplined execution and strategic focus. Phrases such as "we remain steadfast" and "we are prioritizing this proactive approach" signaled confidence, while references to "challenging market environment" and "trough of the cycle" acknowledged difficulties. Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained steady with a focus on operational discipline, though Q&A responses showed more acknowledgment of ongoing uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company shifted its European segment outlook from flat to up 23%-28%, citing Romanian EU stimulus funds as a new tailwind, while Australia's outlook was revised down. Domestic ag revenue guidance and construction guidance were reiterated.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on inventory, government support, and margin pressures, but questions this quarter concentrated more on the timing and magnitude of a potential bottom in the ag cycle.
  • Key metrics such as net loss, revenue, and operating expenses were lower versus the prior quarter, while European revenue growth and margin improvement targets became more prominent.
  • Management's confidence in inventory reduction and margin normalization initiatives increased, but they continued to stress caution amid persistent sector headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing weak farmer profitability, uncertain government support programs, and dry conditions, particularly in Australia, as key challenges.
  • Potential for margin compression in domestic ag and risk of continued sluggish order activity in the back half of the year were highlighted.
  • Analysts raised repeated concerns about the effectiveness and impact of government programs, the sustainability of inventory progress, and the predictability of the ag market trough.

Final Takeaway

Titan Machinery management stressed disciplined execution on inventory reduction and customer care as critical strengths amid a subdued ag environment. The company adjusted its outlook for international segments, notably raising guidance in Europe and lowering it in Australia, while reiterating its domestic ag and construction expectations. Despite a challenging quarter, leadership remains focused on executing strategic initiatives to optimize inventory, improve margins, and position the company for long-term value creation as market conditions evolve.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ball announces CFO departure; appoints Daniel Rabbitt as interim CFO
data.description:
• Ball (NYSE:BALL) announced on Thursday that Howard Yu, the company's chief financial officer, will be stepping down from his role effective June 30, 2025, pursuant to a mutual agreement.
  • "The departure is not related to any disagreement with the company on any matter relating to its accounting practices, financial statements, internal controls or operations," stated the company.
  • The company has appointed Daniel Rabbitt as interim chief financial officer until a permanent successor is named and will update shareholders as appropriate.
  • Rabbitt currently serves as senior vice president of corporate planning and development, a role he has held since 2016. Rabbitt also previously served as vice president and general manager of Ball's Aerosol business.
  • The company reaffirms its previously issued full-year comparable diluted earnings per share growth outlook of 11-14%, and is positioned to generate strong free cash flow, increase EVA and continue long-term return of value to shareholders in 2025.
  • BALL is -1.89% to $52.81.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vanguard: Market volatility in 2025 offers investors a reality check on risk
data.description:
Investors have experienced a turbulent first half of 2025, marked by sharp market swings sparked by early April’s U.S. tariff announcements. Although both equity and fixed income markets managed a relatively quick rebound within a month, the episode served as a timely reminder: market disruptions can emerge quickly and unpredictably Vanguard said.

According to Kevin Khang, a senior international economist at Vanguard, the current environment may signal a shift from the relatively shallow downturns and rapid recoveries that have characterized markets over the past 15 years. “Investors should brace for the possibility that future drawdowns may be more prolonged and severe than what we’ve recently seen,” Khang noted. 

This evolving landscape calls for a more resilient investment approach. Vanguard highlights three guiding principles to help investors navigate uncertainty more effectively.

First, accept that some risks are inherently unknowable and remain humble in the face of market complexity. Second, ensure your portfolio is built on a sound, diversified asset allocation. Finally, maintain cautious optimism—stay hopeful for gains, but prepare for setbacks. 

As 2025 continues to unfold, these principles could prove essential for weathering whatever lies ahead in the global financial markets.

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ).

data.title: New consumption-themed ETF has been filed with SEC
data.description:
A new exchange traded fund, the Alpha Brands Consumption Leaders ETF, has been filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission as it aims to tap into the power of consumer spending and brand dominance across a wide range of industries.

According to the SEC filing, the ETF will be actively managed and carry an annual operating expense ratio of 0.69%. It will draw its holdings from over 70 sub-industries across all sectors, with a focus on companies that are considered current or emerging leaders in consumption-driven markets. 

The fund’s investment strategy is built around a core consumption theme, which is grounded in the belief that household and business spending, particularly on capital expenditures and R&D are key drivers of economic growth. The ETF targets companies that demonstrate strong brand loyalty, technological edge, and high market share, as well as those poised to achieve such status. 

Key sectors are expected to include e-commerce, home improvement retail, and discount apparel and home goods. The fund’s methodology centers on identifying firms with high brand relevance and appeal, aiming to provide investors exposure to both established and rising consumption-centric brands. 

While the SEC filing did not list any direct holdings, some names that may fall into the fund based off its stated objective can be shares of Home Depot (NYSE:HD), Kohl's Corporation (NYSE:KSS), Walmart (NYSE:WMT), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), as well as many others.

data.title: Consumer Discretionary stocks lag in 2025, but these 10 names stand out as strong buys
data.description:
As broader markets continue their recovery from April lows, the consumer discretionary sector remains a notable laggard in the 2025 trading year. Its underperformance has made it the weakest segment of the S&P 500 so far this year, but not all stocks within the sector are being painted with the same brush.

In fact, several large-cap names are still garnering strong bullish sentiment across the board. Below is a curated list of the top 10 consumer discretionary stocks with market capitalizations of $10B or more that are widely regarded as compelling buys. 

Each of these stocks has earned a Buy or Strong Buy rating from Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings, SA Analysts rankings, and Wall Street analysts grades. The list is ranked based on Seeking Alpha’s quant metrics. 

No. 10: Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ:ULTA), quant rating of 3.88.

No. 9: Aptiv PLC (NYSE:APTV), quant rating of 3.90.

No. 8: PDD Holdings (NASDAQ:PDD), quant rating of 4.13.

No. 7: JD.com (NASDAQ:JD), quant rating of 4.21.

No. 6: Sony Group Corporation (NYSE:SONY), quant rating of 4.55.

No. 5: The Gap (NYSE:GAP), quant rating of 4.60.

No. 4: Carnival Corporation & plc (NYSE:CCL), quant rating of 4.76.

No. 3: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), quant rating of 4.80.

No. 2: Alibaba Group Holding Limited (NYSE:BABA), quant rating of 4.83.

No. 1: Prosus N.V. (OTCPK:PROSY), quant rating of 4.84.

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLY), (NYSEARCA:VCR), (NYSEARCA:FXD), (NYSEARCA:FDIS), (NYSEARCA:RSPD), and (NYSEARCA:RXI).

data.title: Mortgage rates rise but remain below the 7% mark
data.description:
Long-term mortgage rates rose this week, but remained below the 7% level for the 18th consecutive week, said the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgage rates averaged 6.86% as of May 22, up from 6.81% last week but down from 6.94% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgage rates averaged 6.01%, up from 5.92% a week ago and down from 6.24% in the year-ago period.

"Mortgage rates inched up this week but continue to remain lower than one year ago," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

"With more inventory for buyers to choose from than the last few years, purchase application activity continues to hold up," added Khater.

data.title: New Google antitrust challenge aims at AI deal: report
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) is reportedly facing still more antitrust scrutiny as it continues investment in artificial intelligence, and the Justice Dept. looks to keep more developed companies from warding off competition in the fast-growing area.

The DOJ is investigating whether Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) technology deal with chatbot maker Character.AI violated antitrust law in an attempt to avoid merger scrutiny, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

That agreement got Google a non-exclusive license for Character.AI's tech, and its founders rejoined Google as well after leaving years ago to start the new company. It's led to an early-stage probe from the Justice Dept. even though the transaction didn't call for a formal review, the report notes.

Google is cooperating and “We’re excited that talent from Character.AI has joined the company but we have no ownership stake and they remain a separate company,” a Google spokesperson said in response, according to the report.

The news opens yet another front in the government's anticompetitive scrutiny of Google. Federal courts have already found the company illegally monopolized online search and ad technology.

Alphabet stock moved somewhat higher Thursday -- (GOOG) +2.7%, (GOOGL) +2.9% -- for the second straight day following Tuesday's Google I/O developer conference, where it revealed its latest innovations in AI tech.

data.title: Analysts stay bullish after AT&T's deal for Lumen's consumer fiber biz
data.description:
Wall Street analysts view AT&T's (NYSE:T) $5.75B purchase of Lumen's (NYSE:LUMN) consumer fiber business as "a positive" and "strategically aligned."

The transaction will benefit both parties as it boosts the U.S. telecom giant's fiber footprint and reduces debt and creates a leaner business structure for the smaller rival.

The deal for AT&T will add about 1M fiber customers and expand coverage by 4M fiber locations across 11 U.S. states. Analysts calculate it to be about $1,300-$1,400 per fiber passing. 

The deal will bring Lumen’s net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio to 3.9x from 4.9x, reduce its capital spending on fiber business by about $1B, improve its annual cash flow, open refinancing opportunities, and sharpen its focus on enterprise fiber business and customers. 

Morgan Stanley says from a competitive perspective, about 85% of the newly bought footprint competes with Comcast (CMCSA) or Charter (CHTR).

The research firm thinks AT&T now has the opportunity to grow the acquired fiber customer base, ramp fiber penetration in the acquired footprint from 25% to 40% over time, and improve its mobility market share in the newly acquired and ultimately converged markets. They continue to stay bullish with an "overweight" rating on the company's growth prospects. 

"This growth is driven by a healthy U.S. wireless industry, improving performance at AT&T Wireless, rapid growth by AT&T Fiber, and ultimately yielding double-digit or low-teens adj. EPS growth. Driving this growth is both broadband market share gains and a rapidly expanding fiber footprint. AT&T, we believe, has the fastest-growing fiber footprint in the world at ~3 mm/year," Morgan Stanley said Thursday.

UBS says the deal would solidify AT&T's position as "the largest converged fiber provider in the U.S." as it targets to reach 60M homes by 2030 vs. Verizon's (VZ) plans for 35-40M and T-Mobile's (TMUS) 12-15M. They continue to rate it a "buy."

UBS sees the transaction as a positive for Lumen and believe the ability to stabilize revenue and EBITDA performance will be key to driving investor sentiment and long-term value accretion for the company.

Bank of America says the transaction is consistent with AT&T’s strategic focus on connectivity, its focus on expanding fiber locations, and its emphasis on using bundling to enhance market share.

"AT&T’s existing business is delivering solid wireless and consumer broadband growth, which we expect will be enhanced and extended by the announced deal...  AT&T planned sufficient financial flexibility to accommodate the opportunity within its $10 billion of flexible capital, which ensures its capital return (dividend/buyback) plans can be maintained," BofA said Thursday. They maintain a "buy" rating.

Analysts at Oppenheimer said they think the deal is "a slight positive" for AT&T and supports its convergence strategy in a relatively small transaction. 

"It reinforces T's position as the largest and fastest fiber builder and adds wireless convergence upside," Oppenheimer said in its note. They maintain "outperform."

data.title: Chip stocks mixed after Analog Devices' Q2 results
data.description:
Chip stocks were mixed on Thursday after Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) reported its second-quarter results.

Shares of Analog Devices fell about 4% despite second-quarter results and third-quarter outlook exceeding analysts' expectations.

Meanwhile, related stocks, Marvell Technology (MRVL) rose about 3%, while Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) was largely flat but among the gainers. Texas Instruments (TXN) fell nearly 2% and Microchip Technology (MCHP) dipped around 1%. Intel (INTC) was largely flat but in the red on Thursday.

AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) rose about 1%. Broadcom (AVGO) was also up around 1%. Qualcomm (QCOM) dipped about 2%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) was also in the red.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) climbed about 1%, while GlobalFoundries (GFS) was largely flat but in the red.

Chip equipment makers: KLA (KLAC) slipped nearly 1%, while Applied Materials (AMAT) was largely flat but in the red. Dutch company ASML (ASML) and Lam Research (LRCX) were also largely flat but among the gainers.

data.title: Lightspeed Commerce stock slides after Q4 earnings fall short, mixed guidance
data.description:
Lightspeed Commerce (NYSE:LSPD) shares gapped down 7% in Thursday late morning trading after delivering worse-than-expected fiscal Q4 2025 earnings as well as mixed guidance for the year.

The fintech company sees FY2026 revenue growth of 10%-12%, (vs. 10.3% average analyst estimate), annual gross profit growth of ~14% (vs. 11.1% Visible Alpha estimate) and annual adjusted EBITDA of $68M-$72M (vs. $72.8M Visible Alpha consensus).

For fiscal Q1 2026, it expects revenue of $185M-$290M, in line with the $284.5M Wall Street consensus; Y/Y gross profit growth of about 13%, exceeding the 10.2% Visible Alpha estimate; and adjusted EBITDA of $14M-$16M, missing the $16.4M Visible Alpha estimate.

"Fiscal 2026 will be dedicated to growing locations, expanding software revenue and enhancing Adjusted EBITDA profitability," said Founder and CEO Dax Dasilva.

Turning to fiscal Q4 2025 results, adjusted EPS of $0.10 slid from $0.12 in the prior quarter and rose from $0.06 a year before, compared with the $0.11 consensus. Including certain items, such as goodwill impairment and share-based compensation, the company recorded a net loss of $0.21 per share.

Total revenue for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, was $253.4M, down from $280.1M in Q3 and advanced from $230.2M in last year's Q4. The latest print surpassed the $251.3M expected.

Total operating expenses surged to $691.9M in Q4 from $149.9M in Q3 and $141.0M a year earlier, mainly reflecting a $556.4M goodwill impairment.

Adjusted EBITDA was $12.9M vs. $16.6M in Q3 and $4.39M in the year-ago period.

Gross profit of $111.8M compared with $115.9M in the previous quarter and gained 12% Y/Y.

In the last 12 months, Lightspeed (NYSE:LSPD) has bought back about 18.7M shares, or ~12% of total shares outstanding, for proceeds of ~$219M.
